qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal via user pictureUri enables arbitrary file read
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding concerns pictureUri path traversal through UserWebService.add and ProfileImageResourceReference, while the advisory concerns the REST login endpoint sending credentials in a GET query string.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Stored XSS via private message body enables session hijacking
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding concerns unsanitized private-message HTML rendered by Wicket setEscapeModelStrings(false), while the advisory concerns the REST login endpoint sending credentials in a GET query string.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described REST login endpoint using HTTP GET with username and password in query parameters. The qualifying findings are separate OpenMeetings issues in profile image file resolution and private-message rendering.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-gcvm-c75m-h4p4 describes CWE-598 in Apache OpenMeetings: the REST login endpoint uses HTTP GET with username and password passed as query parameters.
path_traversal_picture_uri reports UserDTO.pictureUri path traversal through UserWebService.add and ProfileImageResourceReference.
stored_xss_private_message reports unsanitized private message HTML rendered with setEscapeModelStrings(false).
.judge/input_manifest.json lists only path_traversal_picture_uri and stored_xss_private_message as final qualifying_vuln findings.
